blocks.md: You met `⍴` as *shape* (monadic). With a left argument it becomes **reshape** (dyadic): `rows cols ⍴ data` pours the data into a grid of that shape, filling left to right, top to bottom.
If there is less data than the shape needs, APL **recycles** it. This is the easy way to make a constant grid.
The **rank** of an array is how many dimensions it has — the length of its shape. A vector has rank 1, a matrix rank 2. `⍴⍴` (shape of the shape) gives the rank.

blocks.prompt: Make a 3×3 grid of the numbers 1 to 9.
blocks.hint: rows then columns, then the data ⍳9

blocks.prompt: Build a 2×5 grid filled entirely with zeros.
blocks.hint: one value gets recycled to fill the whole shape

blocks.prompt: Reshape the numbers 1 to 6 into a single row of 6 (a 1×6 matrix, not a plain vector).
blocks.hint: a two-element left argument 1 6 forces a matrix

blocks.prompt: Report the rank (number of dimensions) of a 2×3 matrix built from ⍳6.
blocks.hint: rank is the shape of the shape
